---
title: 'T3: A R package for processing tropical tuna fisheries data'
tags:
  - Tuna
  - Species composition
  - surface fisheries
  - Open source software
  - R
authors:
  - name: Mathieu Depetris
    orcid: 0000-0001-8080-0531
    equal-contrib: true
    corresponding: true 
    affiliation: "1, 2" # (Multiple affiliations must be quoted)
  - name: Antoine Duparc
    orcid: 0000-0002-3919-8208
    equal-contrib: true
    corresponding: false 
    affiliation: "1, 2" # (Multiple affiliations must be quoted)
  - name: Pascal Cauquil
    orcid: 0000-0003-0718-8950
    equal-contrib: true
    corresponding: false 
    affiliation: "1, 2" # (Multiple affiliations must be quoted)
  - name: Laurent Floc'h
    orcid: 0000-0003-4764-9766
    equal-contrib: true
    corresponding: false 
    affiliation: "1, 2" # (Multiple affiliations must be quoted)
  - name: Julien Lebranchu
    orcid: 0000-0002-7759-0534
    equal-contrib: true
    corresponding: false
    affiliation: "1, 2" # (Multiple affiliations must be quoted)
affiliations:
 - name: OB7, IRD, Sète, France
   index: 1
 - name: MARBEC, Univ Montpellier, CNRS, IFREMER, IRD, Montpellier, France
   index: 2
#date: 13 August 2017
bibliography: bibliography.bib
# Optional fields if submitting to a AAS journal too, see this blog post:
# https://blog.joss.theoj.org/2018/12/a-new-collaboration-with-aas-publishing
aas-doi: 10.3847/xxxxx <- update this with the DOI from AAS once you know it.
aas-journal: Astrophysical Journal <- The name of the AAS journal.
format: pdf
---

# Summary

The multi-species nature of tropical tuna surface fisheries gives rise to a series of difficulties when estimating the catch by species and catch at size statistics.
The T3 processing was built about 30 years ago in order to correct biases of the logbook data on species composition and to provide more accurate catch estimates per species for the purse seine fleet, specialy European and Seychelles fleet.


# Acknowledgements



# References
